$comment: Hand-authored taxonomy. Powers the Topic Front Door (/t, /t/:topic) and the section list inside each state hub (/s/:state). See ADR-0022 and TODO/IA-RESET-PLACE-FIRST-WITH-TOPIC-FRONT-DOOR.md. DOCTRINE (user-mandated 2026-05-11): elections are ONE indicator family among many. Social-welfare topics (fiscal, education, health, livelihood) are first-class; elections are not the spine and not the default theme. Order in this array IS the rendering order on /t and inside the state hub.

topics.title: Fiscal capacity
topics.summary: How states raise, borrow, and spend. Debt, deficit, transfers from the Centre. The baseline that contextualises every other state-level number.
topics.notes: Debt and deficit are co-determined by central transfers — the transfers indicators (RBI Net via `fiscal/net_transfers_from_centre`, Rajya Sabha Gross via `fiscal/centre_transfers_gross`) are the contextualisation that makes ranked debt/deficit tables honest. Per ADR-0022 ingest gate. RBI Net covers FY24 Accounts + FY25 RE + FY26 BE; Rajya Sabha Gross covers FY17–FY23 (longer historical run, devolution + grants pre-adjustments). The all-India aggregate equivalent (RBI Net Centre→States transfers, FY08–FY26, 19y) is `fiscal/centre_transfers_to_states_net` — a country-entity series for the macro envelope view; its data shape is incompatible with the per-state choropleth (it carries one row per FY for `entity_id="IN"`). The four `fiscal/states_combined_*_deficit` indicators (gross fiscal, revenue, primary, primary revenue) come from RBI Appendix Table 1 (Major Deficit Indicators) — same publication as the transfers, all-states aggregate, FY08–FY26 (20y including RE/BE). They answer the macro question 'how much are the states collectively borrowing each year, and what kind of borrowing is it?'. The matching `fiscal/union_*_deficit` quartet (RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian Economy, Table 89, FY87–FY26 / 40y) ships the Centre's own borrowing as a peer family — without it, ranked deficit tables silently misframe responsibility (cf. Hans's chart-trap warning in docs/concepts/fiscal-actor-naming.md: states-combined GFD ~3% of GDP looks alarming until placed beside the Centre's ~6%).

topics.title: Energy
topics.summary: Where electricity is generated. Installed capacity by state, by fuel.
topics.notes: Installed-MW is a *siting* statistic, not a consumption statistic — the state where a plant sits is not necessarily the state that consumes the power. The indicator artifact's `attribution_geography` field carries this caveat to the renderer. The `installed_capacity_*` family is from CEA's monthly Executive Summary (March 2026 snapshot) and covers all 35 CEA-reported state/UT entities across 7 fuel categories (total, thermal, coal, gas, nuclear, hydro, renewable). The legacy `installed_mw_by_state` indicator (Wikipedia-derived, 4 states only) is retained for back-compat but no longer the default. The state-level family (`state_installed_capacity_*`, `state_electricity_*`, `state_rooftop_solar_capacity_mw`, `state_atc_losses_pct`, `state_acs_arr_gap_inr_per_kwh`) is from the NITI Aayog ICED state-wise deep-dive API and covers 9-11 fiscal years (FY16-FY26) per indicator across all 36 states/UTs plus the All-India aggregate. ICED publishes both 'geographical-location' (where the plant sits) and 'with-allocated-shares' (who has rights to the output) capacity series; we ship both so the analyst can pick the one that matches the question being asked. The 2026-05-15 ICED-power batch adds five complementary series from a different ICED endpoint family (NITI ICED public dashboards, not the state-wise deep-dive): `state_installed_capacity_by_source_mw` (FY16-FY26, 11y, all states × ~7 fuels, 1815 rows — same metric as the state-wise `state_installed_capacity_geographical_mw` but tidy-by-fuel and with longer history), `state_electricity_generation_by_source_gwh` and `state_peak_electricity_demand_mw` (FY26 snapshot from `/energy/powerStatistics`, useful as a current-year companion to the multi-year capacity series), and two national series — `india_thermal_capacity_retired_mw` (FY06-FY26, by retired-fuel facet) and `india_capacity_pipeline_gw` (2011-2031, two pipeline-status facets, including forward-looking commissioning estimates). Pipeline is the only forward-looking energy series we ship and is explicitly flagged as projection in `series_breaks`.

topics.title: Elections
topics.summary: Who governs. State Assembly and Lok Sabha results, candidate-level detail, party totals, margins. One indicator family among many — not the spine of the site.
topics.notes: Per ADR-0022 doctrine: elections are NOT the default home-page theme and NOT a featured topic. Social-welfare topics (fiscal, future: education, health, livelihood) are first-class; elections are one of many. Election artifacts have a different schema family (result.summary.schema.json) than indicators — the catalogue's polymorphic `kind` lets them coexist without being demoted in capability or promoted in IA prominence.

topics.title: Economy
topics.summary: How big is each state's economy, in real and nominal terms. GDP and sectoral GVA at constant and current prices, in Lakh Crore Rupees.
topics.notes: Source: NITI Aayog ICED state-wise deep-dive API (which republishes MoSPI National Statistical Office figures with the 2011-12 base year). Covers 10 fiscal years (FY16-FY25) for all 36 states/UTs plus All-India. Constant-price series strips inflation; current-price series is the nominal 'sticker' value. Compare GDP vs Sectoral GVA: GDP includes net product taxes; GVA is the cleaner production-side measure. The 2025-26 row is typically N.A. while NSO finalises that year's accounts. The per-capita NSDP and per-capita consumption indicators come from the ICED key-economic-indicators API (a separate endpoint family from state-wise deep-dive); they go back to 2004-05 and 2009-10 respectively, longer than the GDP series, because the source publisher provides a longer back-run of those particular metrics. Topic is featured=false pending IA review (the doctrine says social-welfare topics drive IA; pure macro economy is context, not headline).

topics.title: Demography
topics.summary: How many people live where. State population (inter-censal estimates) until the next decadal Census resets the baseline.
topics.notes: Two complementary population series. `state_population_lakhs` is the FY16-FY26 inter-censal projection used for per-capita normalisations on other indicators (denominator role). `state_population_by_sex_count` is the decennial Census enumeration (1961-2011) faceted by sex, useful for sex-ratio analysis and historical context. The most recent Census of India was 2011; the 2021 round was deferred. Topic is featured=false pending the next Census release; until then this is mostly the denominator for per-capita normalisations rather than a headline series.

topics.title: Human development
topics.summary: How states score on the composite of income, health and education — what an average resident's life chances look like, beyond pure income.
topics.notes: Source: NITI Aayog ICED HDI map (sub-national HDI computed by NITI from Indian survey inputs — NFHS for health, NSS/PLFS for education and income). Not directly comparable to UNDP's national HDI for India: input series differ. ICED publishes only two snapshot years (2011-12 and 2017-18) — treat this as a position, not a trajectory. We do not draw a trend line through two points.

topics.title: Environment
topics.summary: What India emits and what burden it puts on the climate system. National greenhouse-gas inventory broken down by sector.
topics.notes: National total only — sub-national emissions accounting does not yet exist for India. Reported in India's Biennial Update Report (BUR) submissions to UNFCCC, methodology per IPCC 2006 guidelines. LULUCF (land-use / land-use-change / forestry) shows up as a net-negative sector when forest absorption exceeds deforestation; that is real, not an error. Per-capita Indian emissions are roughly a quarter of the OECD average; absolute totals reflect a population of 1.4 billion. State-level breakdown remains an open research problem (no government series exists yet).
